page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 7.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance allocates 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2 threads per core. The processor includes a 45 MB L3 cache, 0.25 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache. It supports AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA instruction sets, though it lacks AVX-512 extensions. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate that the n1-highcpu-8 delivers modest processing capabilities. Its single-core and multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng, falls into the bottom 25% tier, with a Geekbench single-core score of 914.0 and a multi-core score of 3557.0. Memory performance is mixed; while the Passmark memory score is weak, the uncached read and write memory bandwidths reach average tiers at 47.28 GB/sec and 34.7 GB/sec, respectively. Cryptographic and physics simulations perform at average levels, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 2707.84. However, text processing and single-threaded compression tasks show poor performance, placing in the bottom 10% of tested systems.

Given its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of approximately 0.9 GB per core, the n1-highcpu-8 is designed for compute-heavy tasks that do not require large memory footprints. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a dense allocation of dedicated vCPUs relative to its memory capacity, making it suitable for workloads where CPU core count is more critical than RAM. Appropriate workloads include basic multi-threaded batch processing, lightweight ray tracing, and moderate database operations. It is less suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale software compilation, or high-throughput web serving due to its constrained memory and weak single-core performance.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance configured with 8 vCPUs and 7.0 GB of RAM, translating to 0.9 GB of memory per core. Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 4 physical cores and hyper-threading, this instance features a 45 MB L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets. It does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the lower tiers for single-core and multi-core CPU execution, though it maintains average memory bandwidth and encryption speeds. Due to its low memory-to-vCPU ratio, the n1-highcpu-8 is qualitatively cost-efficient for CPU-bound tasks with small memory footprints, such as lightweight multi-threaded batch processing, ray tracing, and basic database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 instance with 8 vCPUs, 7.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU. It is designed for compute-focused workloads with low memory requirements.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-8 features 8 vCPUs, 7.0 GB RAM, and a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor for compute-focused workloads.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core compute instance designed for CPU-bound workloads requiring minimal memory overhead and standard cryptographic capabilities.
bullet_points: Features 8 vCPUs and 7.0 GB of RAM on an Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture.
Equipped with 4 physical cores, hyper-threading, and a 45 MB L3 cache.
Delivers average memory bandwidth and encryption performance.
No local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included.
Best suited for lightweight multi-threaded batch processing and ray tracing.
